sources.label: BigGo Finance - Resonac lifts H1 net profit forecast 93% to ¥38B on AI semiconductor materials (May 13, 2026 - confirms catalyst predates Jun 6)
BigGo Finance - Q1 FY2026 earnings call: core OP ¥33.6B (2.3x YoY), H1 guidance raised 40%
Resonac IR / news releases (no material releases since Jun 6; Jun 2 SX Brand selection immaterial)

verdicts.rankRationale: Yesterday's verdict already moved Resonac down for the post-rerating chase; no new information since June 6, so hold at rank 16. Today's +3.7% bounce came on zero fresh news, a mild flow-driven drift against residual alpha, but the ~19% pullback from the May 14 intraday peak (Y20,495 to Y16,560) partially restores asymmetry and offsets a further demotion.
verdicts.bull: Dominant global share in back-end packaging materials (insulation, die-attach, molding compounds) feeding CoWoS/HBM build-outs; May 13 guidance raise (H1 net profit +93% to Y38B, core OP 2.1x to Y74B) shows AI demand running ahead of plan, and conservative full-year guidance (Y140B core OP held) leaves room for another beat at H1 results.
verdicts.neutral: The AI back-end materials story is now fully recognized after consecutive record highs in May; at +145% YTD and ~$19B market cap this is no longer a hidden bottleneck name, though the 11-19% pullback off the highs keeps the setup from being maximally crowded.
verdicts.bear: Legacy petrochemicals/graphite-electrode cyclicality still dilutes the AI mix, and a 145% YTD rerating prices in flawless execution; any HBM/CoWoS order digestion or a second-half guidance disappointment (management itself flagged global uncertainty) triggers an outsized de-rating.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: H1 FY2026 results (Jan-Jun period) and a likely full-year guidance raise off the conservatively held Y140B core operating profit forecast.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Crowding and rerating exhaustion: a widely-owned +145% YTD name where any pause in AI back-end materials volume growth or weak legacy-chemicals pricing compresses the multiple quickly.
